pi_items.metadata_value: Commentaries on the Laws of England
pi_items.verified_value: Commentaries on the Laws of England
pi_items.source: Wikipedia (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Commentaries_on_the_Laws_of_England), Avalon Project (https://avalon.law.yale.edu/subject_menus/blackstone.asp), HathiTrust Digital Library (https://catalog.hathitrust.org/Record/008594325)

Biblio (https://www.biblio.com/book/commentaries-laws-england-four-books-14th/d/480694036), HathiTrust (https://catalog.hathitrust.org/Record/008594325), and historical records confirming the 14th edition was published in 1803 in London by A. Strahan for T. Cadell and W. Davies

Multiple sources confirm William Blackstone (1723-1780) was the original author of Commentaries on the Laws of England, with volumes published 1765-1769. Wikipedia (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/William_Blackstone), Britannica, historical records.

pi_items.verified_value: NOT editor of the 1803 'Commentaries on the Laws of England'. Edward Christian was the editor of the 1803 14th edition. St. George Tucker created a separate American annotated edition titled 'Blackstone's Commentaries: With Notes of Reference to the Constitution and Laws, of the Federal Government of the United States, and of the Commonwealth of Virginia' published by Birch and Small in Philadelphia in 1803.
pi_items.source: Wikipedia (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Commentaries_on_the_Laws_of_England), Biblio listing (https://www.biblio.com/book/commentaries-laws-england-four-books-14th/d/480694036), Lonang Institute (https://lonang.com/library/reference/tucker-blackstone-notes-reference/), Edward Christian Wikipedia (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Edward_Christian), St. George Tucker Wikipedia (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/St._George_Tucker)

privileged_info_summary: The task targets a real historical book: Commentaries on the Laws of England, 14th edition, published in 1803 in London by A. Strahan for T. Cadell and W. Davies. William Blackstone (1723-1780) was the original author and author of the Commentaries. However, the 1803 edition was edited by Edward Christian (1758-1823), not St. George Tucker. St. George Tucker (1752-1827) created a separate American annotated edition with a different title ('Blackstone's Commentaries: With Notes of Reference to the Constitution and Laws, of the Federal Government of the United States, and of the Commonwealth of Virginia') published in Philadelphia in 1803 by Birch and Small. The data references real historical individuals and publications, not synthetic data.
